name: collector
context: Configure, validate, run, and troubleshoot OpenTelemetry Collector receivers, processors, exporters, and pipelines.
info: The OpenTelemetry Collector is a vendor-neutral telemetry agent or gateway. It receives traces, metrics, and logs from SDKs and infrastructure sources; processes telemetry with batching, memory limiting, filtering, enrichment, and sampling; then exports to one or more backends. Agents use this skill to generate collector configs, validate pipeline shape, expose OTLP endpoints, add debug output, configure gateway or agent patterns, and inspect internal telemetry before production rollout.
body:
# OpenTelemetry Collector

The Collector is the operational control plane for telemetry. It is commonly deployed as an agent near workloads, as a central gateway, or as a two-tier agent-to-gateway setup.

## Config Shape

Collector config has top-level component definitions and a `service` section that activates components.

```yaml
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      grpc:
        endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
      http:
        endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
processors:
  memory_limiter:
    check_interval: 1s
    limit_mib: 512
  batch:
exporters:
  debug:
    verbosity: detailed
service:
  extensions: [health_check]
  pipelines:
    traces:
      receivers: [otlp]
      processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
      exporters: [debug]
    metrics:
      receivers: [otlp]
      processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
      exporters: [debug]
```

A component is not active merely because it is defined. It must be referenced by `service.pipelines` or `service.extensions`.

## Generate a Baseline Config

```bash
python3 nodes/opentelemetry/collector/scripts/collector-config.py render --out otelcol.yaml --debug
otelcol validate --config=otelcol.yaml
otelcol --config=otelcol.yaml
```

## Pipeline Components

| Component | Role |
| --- | --- |
| Receiver | Gets telemetry into the Collector, for example `otlp`, `prometheus`, `hostmetrics`, `filelog` |
| Processor | Mutates, batches, limits, filters, samples, enriches, or redacts telemetry |
| Exporter | Sends telemetry out, for example `debug`, `otlp`, `otlphttp`, Prometheus exporter, vendor exporters |
| Connector | Connects pipelines, for example span-derived metrics |
| Extension | Adds operational features like health checks, auth, pprof, zpages |

## Deployment Patterns

Use an agent pattern when each host or pod needs local collection or metadata enrichment. Use a gateway pattern when applications need a stable regional endpoint, centralized sampling, fan-out, or egress control. For tail sampling at scale, keep all spans for a trace on the same collector instance.

sections.title: Config shape
sections.body: Define receivers, processors, exporters, connectors, and extensions at the top level. Activate them in `service.pipelines` and `service.extensions`. Each pipeline is signal-specific: `traces`, `metrics`, or `logs`. The order is receiver -> processors -> exporters.

sections.title: OTLP receiver
sections.body: The `otlp` receiver is the normal SDK ingress. Enable `grpc` on `0.0.0.0:4317` and `http` on `0.0.0.0:4318` when you want both transports. Point SDKs at this receiver with [[opentelemetry/otlp]] environment variables.

sections.title: Processors
sections.body: Use `memory_limiter` early to protect the process, `batch` near the end for efficient export, `resource` or `attributes` for enrichment/redaction, `filter` to drop unwanted telemetry, and `tail_sampling` for policy-based trace retention. Processor availability depends on Collector distribution.

sections.title: Health and debug
sections.body: Enable `health_check` for readiness, inspect Collector logs, and expose internal metrics on the Collector telemetry endpoint. Add the `debug` exporter while verifying app instrumentation and pipeline shape. Remove or lower verbose debug output before high-volume production traffic.

scripts.name: Render or Validate Collector Config
scripts.description: Render a baseline OpenTelemetry Collector config with OTLP receivers, health check, memory limiter, batch processor, debug exporter, and optional OTLP backend exporter; optionally run otelcol validate

OTELCOL_BIN.description: Collector binary used for the validate action

OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT.description: Optional backend OTLP endpoint for rendered exporter config

action.description: Render a config or validate an existing config with otelcol

backendEndpoint.description: Optional OTLP backend endpoint for exporter

pipelines.description: Pipeline names included in the config

valid.description: Validation result when action is validate

depends.reason: The baseline Collector config receives telemetry through OTLP
Processors often enrich or filter resource attributes
